items.title: One-hot, ordinal, and target encoding definitions
items.description: Canonical definitions, formulas, and leakage pitfalls for one-hot, ordinal, and target encoding live in Encoding Categoricals. This topic applies those methods inside pipelines and cardinality-driven workflows without re-deriving the core rules.

items.title: Transformer pipeline contract
items.description: Categorical encoders follow the same fit-on-train, transform-on-test Transformer API as text vectorizers and FeatureHasher. ColumnTransformer composes tabular encoding branches beside extraction steps in one serializable artifact.

items.title: Tree models consume encoded numerics
items.description: Classic CART implementations require numeric matrices—categoricals arrive as one-hot columns or native categorical dtypes. Encoding workflow choices determine whether splits are threshold-based on dummies or optimal per category level.

items.title: Leakage from misfit encoders
items.description: Fitting encoders on train+test, computing in-sample target statistics, or refitting vocabularies on production batches leaks information into features—the same evaluation failure mode as scaling before splitting.
